top_issues.title: 1.2 PureTech petrol engine carbon buildup and premature failure
top_issues.description: The 1.2 PureTech petrol engine is prone to excessive carbon deposits on the intake valves, leading to engine warning lights, starting problems, and in some cases complete engine failure requiring replacement. This has occurred at relatively low mileages (14,500–27,000 miles) even on cars serviced to schedule. The root cause appears linked to the engine's design and fuel quality; Peugeot has not always advised owners to use premium unleaded fuel exclusively, which may accelerate the problem.
typical_symptoms: Engine warning light illuminated on dashboard
Difficulty starting or failure to start
Fault codes related to throttle control (P0638, P2173)
Excessive oil consumption (1 litre per 2,000–4,000 miles)
Loss of power or rough running

top_issues.title: Manual gearbox first gear popping out under load
top_issues.description: Both the 2017 and 2020 2.0 HDi manual models have been reported to pop out of first gear during normal driving. This is a gearbox synchroniser or selector mechanism fault that makes the car unsafe and difficult to drive, particularly in traffic or on hills. Repair typically requires gearbox removal and overhaul or replacement.
typical_symptoms: Gearbox slips out of first gear while driving

top_issues.title: Electrical system faults and software glitches
top_issues.description: Several owners have reported recurring electrical problems including stop/start system failure, satnav malfunction, and dashboard rattles linked to electrical issues. Some faults appear temperature-sensitive or related to battery charge state. Early 1.2 PureTech models also had software issues with the massage seat function defaulting to 'on' and requiring multiple button presses to disable. Peugeot has issued software update campaigns for some engine control units.
typical_symptoms: Massage seat activates automatically and cannot be easily switched off
Intermittent electrical faults that reappear after short periods

top_issues.title: Oil pump failure and insufficient oil pressure on 1.2 PureTech engines
top_issues.description: Some 1.2 PureTech engines have developed faults with the oil pump intermittently failing to produce sufficient oil pressure, triggering the orange engine management light. The fault is not always immediately obvious and may require the engine to be stripped down for diagnosis. This is a serious issue as low oil pressure can lead to engine damage if not addressed promptly.
typical_symptoms: Light may extinguish after engine restart
No other obvious symptoms until engine damage occurs

top_issues.title: Diesel engine oil contamination and camshaft oil leaks
top_issues.description: The 1.5 BlueHDi diesel engine has been reported to suffer from oil contamination at very low mileages (around 4,000 miles), requiring an unscheduled oil change. Additionally, some 2018 models have developed oil leaks from the camshaft seal, and dealers have been reluctant to cover the repair cost under warranty. These issues suggest potential design or manufacturing quality concerns with the diesel engine.
typical_symptoms: Oil level warning or service reminder at unusually low mileage
Visible oil leak from engine bay, particularly around camshaft area
Dark or contaminated oil on dipstick check

overall_reliability_note: The 2016–2024 Peugeot 3008 is a stylish and well-equipped family SUV, but early 1.2 PureTech petrol engines have shown a concerning tendency towards carbon buildup and premature failure, whilst manual gearbox and electrical gremlins have affected some examples. Diesel variants and automatic transmissions have generally been more reliable, though oil contamination and seal leaks have been reported.
